task:
Create a product context file for the following product:

Product: TaskFlow — a project management tool for remote engineering teams.
Target customer: Engineering managers at 20–100 person startups.
Key differentiator: Real-time dependency mapping that auto-updates when tasks slip.
Main competitor: Linear and Jira.
Customer quote from G2: 'I used to spend every Monday morning manually updating our roadmap after the weekend sprint. TaskFlow does it automatically — I got that hour back every week.'
Current conversion action: Free trial signup.
Current MRR goal: Grow trial-to-paid from 15% to 25%.
expect: Produces a complete product-context.md file covering all 12 sections (overview, category, audience, personas, pain points, competitive positioning, differentiators, objections, customer language, brand voice, proof points, business goals)
Customer language section includes the verbatim G2 quote and identifies it as high-value copy asset
Competitive positioning table compares to Linear and Jira with specific differentiators
Business goal section reflects the trial-to-paid conversion target

task:
The following product-context.md file exists but is incomplete. Audit it and identify the gaps, then ask clarifying questions to fill the most important ones.

```
# Product Marketing Context
## Product Overview
Invoicing software for freelancers.
## Target Audience
Freelancers and small business owners.
## Pain Points
Invoicing is painful.
## Customer Language
[None yet]
```
expect: Identifies that customer language section is empty (the most critical gap)
Notes that 'freelancers and small business owners' is too broad — asks for behavioural specificity
Notes that 'invoicing is painful' is too vague — asks for specific pain scenarios
Proposes at least 2 methods to find customer language (G2 reviews, Reddit mining, customer interviews)
Asks focused, one-at-a-time clarifying questions rather than dumping all questions at once

task:
A marketing specialist discovered these new customer quotes during a recent campaign and wants to update the product context file. The quotes are:
1. 'We switched from HubSpot because we were paying for features our team of 5 would never use'
2. 'Setup took 20 minutes — our old CRM took 3 weeks to configure'
3. 'The Slack integration means I never have to log into the CRM to see deal status'

The current context file has customer language section but no quotes about setup speed or the Slack integration.
expect: Adds all three verbatim quotes to the customer language section of the context file
Updates the competitive positioning section to reflect the HubSpot comparison (switching from HubSpot)
Notes setup speed (20 minutes vs. 3 weeks) as a new differentiator and adds it to the differentiators section
Notes the Slack integration as a key feature and checks whether it's reflected in the feature/differentiators section
